delete.summary:
Attempts to delete a message. Depending on the type of channel and the desired "deleteForEveryone" behavior, these things might happen:
1. The message needs to be deleted only in out database and is deleted immediately within this action. (Business channel, delete for me).
2. The message cannot be deleted because the remote locations where the message should also be deleted no longer accept to be deleted because of time restraints (Starter channel, delete for everyone).
3. The remote location where the message should also be deleted does not allow messages to deleted at all (Business channel, delete for everyone).
4. The message can be deleted in the remote location, but the only thing the action does is request it. Deletion from our database only happens when we receive a confirmation that the message was actually deleted (Starter channel, delete for me or everyone).

features.description:
Describes the features available to an organization based on their contract plan and the organization creation date.

Supports only some primitive and other simple values:
<list type="bullet">
  <item>
    bool
  </item>
  <item>
    int
  </item>
  <item>
    float
  </item>
  <item>
    string
  </item>
  <item>Any System.Enum</item>
</list>

Remarks for using enums:
<list type="bullet">
  <item>Does not (yet) support Flag enums</item>
  <item>Values are stored as their underlying type. So they can be renamed, but not have their value reassigned or reordered</item>
  <item>Values must start at 0 and must not have gaps. Otherwise values after a gap will have a different meaning for the frontend, as the enumeration itself there is assumed. It's a limitation of OpenAPI</item>
  <item>It is recommended to include a comment reference in any enums used here to point back to this documentation to prevent any incidents</item>
</list>

  <item>Any System.Enum</item>
</list>

Remarks for using enums:
<list type="bullet">
  <item>Does not (yet) support Flag enums</item>
  <item>Values are stored as their underlying type. So they can be renamed, but not have their value reassigned or reordered</item>
  <item>Values must start at 0 and must not have gaps. Otherwise values after a gap will have a different meaning for the frontend, as the enumeration itself there is assumed. It's a limitation of OpenAPI</item>
  <item>It is recommended to include a comment reference in any enums used here to point back to this documentation to prevent any incidents</item>
</list>
